page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 11 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the c8i-flex.12xlarge demonstrates strong performance across multiple domains. Its Intel Xeon processor features advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to strong scores in extended instructions and encryption tests. Single-core and multi-core Geekbench scores are highly competitive, with image and text processing reaching top-tier performance. Memory bandwidth is also notable, with cached small-block read speeds reaching 3838.93 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 193.31 GB/sec.

Given its compute-centric design and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is optimized for compute-heavy workloads that require high-speed processing without massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference, as evidenced by its strong performance in LLM prompt processing and text generation for small to medium models. The flex-class designation indicates a balance of dedicated compute resources and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that do not require continuous maximum utilization of all hardware features.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 11 Gbps. Benchmark results highlight strong performance in software compilation, cryptography, and image processing, alongside top-tier text processing and small-model LLM inference. Supported by the Nitro hypervisor, the instance utilizes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This hardware profile makes the c8i-flex.12xlarge highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads, including database operations, web serving, and parallel processing tasks that do not require massive memory allocations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance for compilation and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 48 vCPUs, DDR5 memory, and advanced Intel matrix extensions for high-throughput processing workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor.
96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz.
Advanced instruction support including Intel AMX and AVX-512.
11 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration.
Strongly suited for software compilation, database operations, and web serving.
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included.
